title: document missing commands between docker and kpod
body:
The list was incomplete. Some commands implemented in both were
missing from the main table. We also add a list of commands
implemented only in Docker, and only in kpod.

Signed-off-by: Antoine Beaupré <anarcat@debian.org>

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

:pushpin: Commit 0e2d4aa has been approved by `mheon`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=mheon 0e2d4aaeb0a5c890724df1543f66e242c75fd55a -->

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/39b697d9afb4f4a08f16eee58690e61c897bf1aa.1513092310668313344/index.html)
Approved by: mheon
Pushing 39b697d9afb4f4a08f16eee58690e61c897bf1aa to master...

On 2017-12-12 15:15:06, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> LGTM.  Just wondering if we need a "Docker commands that will NOT be supported" column or are there plans to get them all up?  I was questioning `docker swarm` for one.  I'm fine with merging now though. 

I agree: that's something I'd like to see as well, but that's a policy
decision rather than just a documentation issue, something I cannot
answer myself. :)
Yes I will Open A PR on that.  No intention of supporting Swarm  :^)
